name: Cable Wood-Chop
resistance_profile: Rotational core exercise: drive the cable diagonally across the body — trains the obliques through loaded trunk rotation.
cues: Pivot the back foot and rotate through the trunk, not the arms
Keep the arms fairly straight — power comes from the core
Control the return; don't let it yank you back
execution_steps: Set the cable to shoulder height and stand side-on to the stack with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the handle with both hands and extend your arms out in front of your chest.
Rotate your trunk away from the stack, pivoting your back foot as you turn.
Keep your arms fairly straight and drive the rotation from your core, not your shoulders.
Control the return slowly to the start, finish all reps, then switch sides.
good_when: You want to train the obliques through loaded, dynamic trunk rotation.
You want rotational carryover for sports like throwing or swinging.
You already have core stability and want to add a rotation pattern.
bad_when: You tend to rotate from the lower back rather than the trunk and hips.
You want direct rectus abdominis (six-pack) work (use a crunch).
You're a beginner still learning to brace (start with a Pallof press).
common_errors: Bending the arms and turning it into an arm exercise
Rotating from the lower back instead of the trunk
